dataList.dlg:
"A mass outbreak of Distortion shares a common"... uh, common...
Tsk. I know I read this part in the manual. Common... Ah... It's gone—I can't remember.

... I still can't get used to waking at an hour this late, after idling in bed through the early hours of the day. Back during my life in the Blade Lineage, I would rise before daybreak and set out as my feet would carry me.
Here in the LCD, though, one of my seniors seems averse to the idea of getting to work so early in the morning—so I make sure to report in right on the dot.

I know well enough that excess food dulls the body, so I always try to watch what I'm eating... But it seems rather inappropriate to refuse every sweet treat my seniors offer me.
... Besides, I've never had the chance to taste anything so sweet and scrumptious back home. That's another reason that it's not easy for me to say "No, thank you."

Oh... No, I'm done with work. I was simply committing what I'd learned today to memory—learning something new tends to keep me awake at night.
The way of a Salsu, who carves out the path forward by the force of one's blade, is nothing like this company's directive... I suspect the forthcoming lessons won't be easy to digest for me.

My mentor's mind... is yet to regain its former tranquility. I've much to discuss with him regarding the Blade Lineage and S Corp., but he doesn't seem eager to broach the subject, so I have no choice but to wait indefinitely for his recovery. Until then, I'll do what I must, remaining by his side to offer him what assistance I can. As for what comes after... that's another mystery.

Resting in warm quarters surrounded by kind and caring seniors... It's a frightening amazement how fast complacency sets in. I'd thought my resolve to return to S Corp. was unshakable, but even that rock-ribbed determination seems to falter as I spend more days in comfort.
In times like this, I must remind myself... not to forget. To try all the harder to hold onto the purpose I carried in my heart when I left S Corp.

Those who sought only the liberty to brandish their swords with abandon have deserted us, while those who shared with us the commitment to someday return to S Corp. had their lives cut short by none other than the sword of their very own mentor.
Only I alone... remain unashamed to use the name "Blade Lineage."

These prosthetic arms the Phenomena Deciphering Team provided me were already more than what I could have asked for, and now they've even gifted me such a fine sword... I never thought I'd be shown such generosity when I'm already relying on their hospitality.
I intend to make myself useful and earn everything they've given me—and more. I refuse to be the sort of person who takes kindness for granted.

My battle prowess falls short of that of my seniors, nor am I as perceptive or intelligent as the Lady Team Leader.
I draw the arc of my blade along the trajectory of my life, I cut down what must be felled, and spill the lifeblood of that which must be killed—human and Distortion alike. For no other reason than that this is how a Salsu must carry herself.

The winds may wear me down, and the rains may weather me hollow, but the edge of my blade shall never dull. Therefore, I will take up my burden as I am bidden to carry myself.
*exhale* LCD On-site Investigative Reasoning Team Ishmael—now commencing Distortion capture as directed.
Would you mind keeping a little distance when you speak? I was told that pacing about distractedly and crowding around the monitor in confusion are strictly forbidden.
... We're leaving already? Oh, but I haven't finished perusing the manual—Ah, coming!
*exhale* Light the lantern and open the path ahead.
... Step back, please. You are inviting undue danger by coming close.
Draw the sword... and fell!
When a foe's points of liberty are all chained...
This may be the time to fall back...
Trap the stones and capture them all!
Straight and true... was my blade. That alone... is enough.
It's all thanks to your teachings, seniors.
I still have much to learn...
Suppression complete. This should fulfill the terms of the contract, but now we will never know how that soul Distorted into such a form.

... They're coming back. Back from the Distorted form to that of a human. The way of a Salsu would never have achieved this outcome.
I finally understand what I must learn here in the OSIR Team before I depart for S Corp.

Our stones on the board are driven to fatal positions, cornered on every side. We ought to devise a proper strategem to counter this... Oh. A Letter of Explanation and Apology? Oh, no, I'll write it, but it's just that... I... I've never had to write one before... Um, all right. I'll submit it at once if you give me the format.
